page: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the Ddsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C x86_64 processor. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage capabilities include a bundled 5669 GB local disk with Premium Storage support, while no dedicated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor includes a large 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including VNNI and BF16, and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, specifically AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core performance, achieving a top-tier PassMark CPU score of 106,546.32 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 97,236.44. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core results falling in the average tier.

Memory subsystem performance is highly efficient, with cached read-only small block bandwidth reaching 7,269.34 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth at 348.8 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. The instance excels in data-intensive operations, recording 156.15 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and a top-tier PassMark database operations score of 33,116.64. Additionally, the hardware-assisted matrix instructions enable top-tier prompt processing speeds for medium and large language models, though text generation speeds remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D96ds_v7 offers a high-density resource profile, combining substantial compute capacity with a massive local storage allocation. This balance makes it suitable for enterprise database hosting, high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv7 family, powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 384.0 GB of RAM and 5669 GB of local storage. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, this x86_64 instance features advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 106,546.32, and exceptional memory read bandwidth of up to 7,269.34 GB/sec. The server delivers strong performance in database operations, achieving 156.15 million operations per second in Redis SET tests, and exhibits top-tier prompt processing speeds for medium and large language models. While single-core CPU performance and text generation speeds are average, the high-density compute and storage configuration makes it highly effective for enterprise databases, multi-threaded data processing, and static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v7 is a 96-vCPU general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon processor, 384 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v7 is a 96-vCPU general-purpose instance with 384 GB RAM, 5669 GB local storage, and top-tier database performance.
tagline: A high-density general-purpose instance combining ninety-six Intel vCPUs, massive local storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs and 384 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 6973P-C
Bundled 5669 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier memory read bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support accelerating LLM prompt processing
Excellent fit for enterprise databases, Redis deployments, and web serving
